name: create_article
description: Create a new help center article in your Intercom workspace. Provide the article title, body content in HTML format, and the author's admin ID. Optionally place the article within a collection or section, set its publication state, and configure translations. Use this to build self-service documentation, FAQs, and knowledge base content for customers.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The title of the article. Should be concise and descriptive for search discoverability.

parameters.name: author_id
parameters.description: The ID of the admin who is the author of the article. The author's name appears on the published article.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The article body in HTML format. Supports headings (h2-h4), paragraphs, bold, italic, links, images, lists, code blocks, and tables.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: A short description of the article content. Used in search results and article cards.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Publication state: 'published' (visible to customers) or 'draft' (only visible to admins). Default is 'draft'.

parameters.name: parent_id
parameters.description: The ID of the parent collection or section to place this article in. If omitted, the article is created without a parent.

parameters.name: parent_type
parameters.description: The type of parent: 'collection' or 'section'. Required when parent_id is provided.

body.title: How to Set Up Two-Factor Authentication
body.body:
Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)

Two-factor authentication adds an extra layer of security to your account. Follow these steps to enable it:

Step 1: Go to Security Settings

Navigate to Settings > Security in your dashboard.

Step 2: Enable 2FA

Click the Enable Two-Factor Authentication toggle. You'll be prompted to scan a QR code with your authenticator app.

Step 3: Verify Your Setup

Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to confirm the setup.

Supported Authenticator Apps

  • Google Authenticator
  • Authy
  • 1Password
  • Microsoft Authenticator

If you lose access to your authenticator, contact support with your recovery code.
body.description: Step-by-step guide to enabling 2FA on your account for improved security
